errors.fillAll: Please fill in all fields before continuing.

moderate.title: MODERATE PHYSICAL ACTIVITY
moderate.body: Activity you can do while talking, with some difficulty while moving, but without being able to sing. (Answers based on the last 7 days.)

qPrefix.weekdays: On how many weekdays did you perform moderate physical activity? (Mon–Fri) And for how long? (hours:minutes)
qPrefix.weekend: On how many weekend days did you perform moderate physical activity? And for how long? (hours:minutes)

vigorous.title: VIGOROUS PHYSICAL ACTIVITY
vigorous.body: Activity where you cannot even talk while doing it. Breathing becomes faster than normal and heart rate increases a lot. (Answers based on the last 7 days.)

qPrefix.weekdays: On how many weekdays did you perform vigorous physical activity? (Mon–Fri) And for how long? (hours:minutes)
qPrefix.weekend: On how many weekend days did you perform vigorous physical activity? And for how long? (hours:minutes)

sedentary.title: SEDENTARY BEHAVIOUR
sedentary.body: Activities such as: watching TV, using the phone, using the computer, doing crafts/manual work, driving, reading, staying seated, lying down or reclined. (Answers based on the last 7 days.)

qPrefix.weekdays: On how many weekdays did you engage in sedentary behaviour? (Mon–Fri) And for how long? (hours:minutes)
qPrefix.weekend: On how many weekend days did you engage in sedentary behaviour? And for how long? (hours:minutes)

result.title: Result
result.subtitle: Sleep, physical activity and sedentary behaviour

noData.label: No data provided
noData.description: Enter the usual sleep time to generate the interpretation.

below.label: Below recommended
below.description: Daily average below {{minH}} h. In older adults, this deserves attention because short sleep may worsen recovery, mood, cognition and physical performance.

above.label: Above the recommended range
above.description: Daily average above {{maxH}} h. This is not a diagnosis; it may reflect individual needs, naps, illness, low activity, or recall error.

within.label: Within recommendation
within.description: Daily average compatible with the reference range for {{ageText}}.

upper.label: Meets WHO upper range
upper.description: Reaches 300 min/week or more in moderate-equivalent minutes, a range associated with additional benefits when there is no contraindication.

minimum.label: Meets WHO minimum recommendation
minimum.description: Reaches at least 150 min/week of moderate activity, or equivalent with vigorous activity.

below.label: Below WHO recommendation
below.description: There is some activity, but still below the weekly minimum recommended for substantial health benefits.

none.label: No moderate/vigorous activity reported
none.description: The questionnaire did not record moderate or vigorous physical activity in the week.

noData.label: No data provided
noData.description: Enter sedentary time to generate the interpretation.

low.label: Low/moderate
low.description: Below thresholds where risk starts to appear more consistently in sitting-time studies.

attention.label: Attention
attention.description: Intermediate range. A practical recommendation is to replace part of this time with active breaks and light activity.

high.label: High
high.description: Above roughly 9 h/day, meta-analyses in older adults already observe a relevant increase in risk.

veryHigh.label: Very high
veryHigh.description: Above 10 h/day. Mortality and sedentary behaviour studies point to a worse risk profile in this range.

result.disclaimer: Criteria based on frequency, duration and an estimated MET-min/week (moderate=4 METs, vigorous=8 METs).

alerts.shareUnavailable: Sharing is not available on this device.
alerts.sentBody:
Session S{{session}} sent to the cloud.

{{path}}

weeklyTime.title: Weekly time
weeklyTime.subtitle: Weekly totals and daily average (168 h/week).
weeklyTime.overflowWarning: Sum above 168 h/week by {{overflow}}. Light time was adjusted to 0.

sedentary.title: Sedentary behaviour
sedentary.subtitle: Practical cut-offs: attention above 7 h/day; high above 9 h/day.

sedentary.title: Sedentary
sedentary.body: Did not perform any physical activity for at least 10 continuous minutes or did not meet the criteria for the active levels.

irregular_b.title: Irregularly active B
irregular_b.body: Performs physical activities, but does not meet either the frequency (days) or duration (time) criteria of category “A” or “Active”.

irregular_a.title: Irregularly active A
irregular_a.body: Performs physical activities, but does not meet all the “Active” criteria (e.g., 5 days or 150 min/week).

active.title: Active
active.body: a) Vigorous activity: ≥ 3 days/week + total volume ≥ 1500 MET-min/week; OR b) 7 days of (Walking + Moderate + Vigorous) ≥ 3000 MET-min/week.

very_active.title: Very active
very_active.body: a) Vigorous activity: ≥ 3 days/week + 1500 MET-min/week; OR b) 5 days of (Walking + Moderate + Vigorous) ≥ 3000 MET-min/week.

fesi.subtitle: Falls Efficacy Scale — International
fesi.concernPrompt: How concerned are you about falling while doing this activity?

result.scoreHint: Possible range: 16 to 64 points. The higher the score, the greater the concern/fear of falling.
result.missingParticipant: The participant was not passed to the result screen. Pass the participant object in navigation.

items.q1: Cleaning the house (e.g. dusting, vacuuming, or sweeping).
items.q6: Sitting down and standing up from a chair.
items.q9: Reaching for something above your head or on the ground.
items.q10: Answering the telephone before it stops ringing.
items.q11: Walking on a slippery surface (e.g. wet floor).
items.q13: Walking in a place with lots of people.
items.q14: Walking on an uneven surface (e.g. rocky ground, potholed).
items.q15: Walking up or down a slope.
items.q16: Going to a social event (e.g. religious service, family gathering, or club meeting).

errors.idadeNoDob: No date of birth on file. Select an age bracket.
errors.mobilidadeWeight: Q14: indicate whether there was weight loss (Yes/No).
errors.mobilidadeBmi: Q14: enter weight and height (to calculate BMI).
errors.mobilidadeWalk: Q14: perform the 4 m walk test (stopwatch required).
errors.comorbidades: Select “None of the options below” or at least one condition.
errors.comorbidadesPick: Q20: select at least one option (or “None”).
errors.comorbidadesExclusive: Q20: “None” cannot be selected together with other options.

idade.noValidDob: No valid date of birth on file. Select:

percepcao.intro: In general, compared with other people your age, would you say your health is:

avd_i.hint: Note: this section has a maximum score of 4 points.
avd_i.q3: Q3. Because of your health or physical condition, have you stopped doing shopping?
avd_i.q4: Q4. Because of your health or physical condition, have you stopped managing your money, spending or paying household bills?
avd_i.q5: Q5. Because of your health or physical condition, have you stopped doing light housework (washing dishes, tidying or light cleaning)?

avd_b.q6: Q6. Because of your health or physical condition, have you stopped bathing alone?

cognicao.q7: Q7. Has any relative or friend said you are becoming forgetful?
cognicao.q8: Q8. Is this forgetfulness getting worse in recent months?
cognicao.q9: Q9. Is this forgetfulness preventing you from doing any daily activity?

humor.q10: Q10. In the last month, have you felt down, sad or hopeless?
humor.q11: Q11. In the last month, have you lost interest or pleasure in activities you used to enjoy?

mobilidade.hint: Note: Q14 scores at most 2 points (even if several criteria are positive).
mobilidade.q12: Q12. Are you unable to raise your arms above shoulder level?
mobilidade.q13: Q13. Are you unable to handle or hold small objects?
mobilidade.weightLossHint: 4.5 kg or 5% in the last year, or 6 kg in the last 6 months, or 3 kg in the last month.
mobilidade.q15: Q15. Do you have walking difficulty that prevents you from doing any daily activity?
mobilidade.q16: Q16. Have you had two or more falls in the last year?

continencia.q17: Q17. Do you lose urine or faeces unintentionally at any time?

comunicacao.q18: Q18. Do you have vision problems that prevent you from doing any daily activity?
comunicacao.q18Hint: Glasses or contact lenses are allowed.
comunicacao.q19: Q19. Do you have hearing problems that prevent you from doing any daily activity?

comorbidades.hint: Maximum score for Q20: 4 points (if any condition is positive).
comorbidades.meds: Regular use of five or more different medicines every day
comorbidades.hosp: Recent hospital admission in the last six months

result.title: IVCF-20 result
result.scoreHint: The higher the score, the greater the clinical-functional vulnerability.
result.pizzaHint: Each slice fills from the centre outward by (score ÷ domain maximum).
result.uploadMissingParticipant: Participant was not passed to the result screen. Pass the participant object in navigation.
result.uploadFailBody: Failed to send questionnaire to the cloud.
